issues.description: Plan modifies app/risk/manager.py, explicitly listed as unauthorized. Current RiskManager state and gates are in app/risk/manager.py:44, app/risk/manager.py:102, app/risk/manager.py:215, and app/risk/manager.py:234.
issues.fix_proposed: Remove PR-J from this plan or split it into a separately authorized risk plan. For this plan, prevent double-exposure inside app/execution/engine.py only, by enforcing one non-arbitrage signal per event before risk checks.

issues.location: PR-B step 4, PR-C step 3, PR-D step 2, PR-E step 2, PR-F step 2, PR-G step 2, PR-H step 2, PR-I step 2
issues.description: Plan repeatedly modifies app/core/dependencies.py to wrap and unwrap strategies, but allowed production scope excludes app/core/. Existing strategy registration lives in app/core/dependencies.py:218-243.
issues.fix_proposed: Either get explicit scope approval for app/core/dependencies.py, or keep wrapping inside app/strategies/registry.py without changing dependency wiring.

issues.description: Plan modifies app/core/yaml_config.py and config/config.example.yaml for max_event_exposure_pct, but allowed scope excludes app/core/ and config/. Current risk config is app/core/yaml_config.py:71-85 and config/config.example.yaml:21-27.
issues.fix_proposed: Remove config changes from this plan. If event exposure is handled later, create a separate authorized risk/config plan.

issues.description: Plan may create app/models/event.py, but maximal model scope only includes app/models/market.py. A7 leaves Event model placement undecided while PR-A tests depend on an Event return type.
issues.fix_proposed: Use a lightweight Event model in app/models/market.py or a private typed structure in app/clients/polymarket_rest.py, or explicitly request scope expansion for app/models/event.py before freeze.

issues.description: Plan identifies groups from Gamma /markets inline eventId/events fields and only later falls back to /events. Fixed constraint says group identification must be ONLY via Polymarket Gamma /events API. Current _parse_market only parses market payload fields at app/clients/polymarket_rest.py:54-137.
issues.fix_proposed: Do not use /markets inline eventId/events for grouping. Populate group event_id only from records returned by GET /events or GET /events/{id}; direct inline fields may be logged for diagnostics but must not drive grouping.

issues.description: If /events is empty, stale, rate-limited, or returns 503 after retries, PR-B silently puts markets with event_id == "" into singleton groups. That can trade multiple markets from the same event and defeats the objective. Retry behavior exists in app/clients/polymarket_rest.py:250-304, but the plan has no fail-closed grouping policy.
issues.fix_proposed: Distinguish true standalone markets from grouping-unknown markets. When /events lookup fails or is inconsistent, mark grouping_incomplete and skip trading those markets for the tick, with tests for 429, 503, empty events, and missing condition_id joins.

issues.description: list_events(active=True, limit=100, offset=0) has no pagination loop. Active Polymarket events can exceed 100, so joins by condition_id can miss later events and degrade to singleton groups.
issues.fix_proposed: Specify offset pagination until a page returns fewer than limit, with a hard page cap and logging. Add tests where the needed event appears after offset 100.

issues.description: Event parsing and market join keys are underspecified: eventId, event, events, id, conditionId, and nested markets may conflict. Current Market has both id and condition_id at app/models/market.py:67-69, and _parse_market maps conditionId at app/clients/polymarket_rest.py:131.
issues.fix_proposed: Freeze an explicit schema contract: direct event.id, event.slug, nested markets[*].id, markets[*].conditionId. Define conflict precedence, log inconsistent mappings, and skip ambiguous joins instead of guessing.

issues.description: Engine refactor calls self._market_service.group_by_event(markets, valuations) or maybe get_grouped_markets, but MarketService currently has no group_by_event and MarketScanner also has no group_by_event. Existing flat fetch is app/execution/engine.py:122 and app/services/market_service.py:76-100.
issues.fix_proposed: Define one concrete API before implementation: either MarketService.get_grouped_markets returns groups and engine flattens for valuation, or app/services/market_scanner.py exposes group_by_event(markets). Remove the 'oppure' branch.

issues.description: A5 proposes top_K=20 for large events, but no PR step applies the cap before VAE assessment. The engine assesses all markets before signal grouping at app/execution/engine.py:180-184, so N>50 events still incur full valuation cost.
issues.fix_proposed: Group and cap before assess_batch, then pass only selected markets to VAE. Add tests for an event with more than 50 markets proving only top_K by volume_24h/id are assessed.

issues.location: PR-B step 6 and PR-C through PR-I
issues.description: Plan makes each strategy choose one winner per group, but engine still iterates all applicable strategies and appends every strategy output. Current engine loops strategies at app/execution/engine.py:222-245. This can produce multiple non-arbitrage trades on the same event_id in one tick.
issues.fix_proposed: Add an engine-level final arbiter: after collecting strategy outputs, group non-arbitrage signals by event_id and keep one winner by deterministic priority score. Allow arbitrage list[Signal] only for its own exception.

issues.description: LegacyStrategyAdapter returns the first non-None signal. That is not behavior-preserving versus the current per-market loop, and it can select a lower-edge market purely because it appears first. The current engine previously generated every per-market signal at app/execution/engine.py:213-245 and sorted later.
issues.fix_proposed: Have the adapter evaluate all markets with valuations, then deterministically pick the best signal using the same priority formula as app/execution/engine.py:250-263 or a documented edge/confidence score. Test ties by market id.

issues.description: PR-B wraps all 7 strategies, including arbitrage, with an adapter that returns the first non-None result. Arbitrage currently returns two-leg lists from app/strategies/arbitrage.py:94-114 and app/strategies/arbitrage.py:136-156. Wrapped arbitrage would stop after the first mispriced market in a group, violating the fixed arbitrage exception.
issues.fix_proposed: Do not use first-non-None adapter for arbitrage. Migrate arbitrage in PR-B or create a special legacy adapter that aggregates list[Signal] outputs across all markets.

issues.location: PR-F step 4, PR-G, PR-H, Ambiguita A4 and A10
issues.description: KnowledgeContext source remains unresolved while PR-F/G/H require it. Current engine only injects external_signals into VAE at app/execution/engine.py:148-181; strategies receive no knowledge at app/execution/engine.py:225. A4 offers incompatible options, including changing the Protocol, after the fixed signature is already set.
issues.fix_proposed: Before PR-F, choose one concrete source compatible with the fixed signature, e.g. knowledge from the most-liquid market in the group, and add tests for missing, stale, and mixed knowledge contexts.

issues.location: Out of scope SELL->BUY-NO note and Assunzione 6
issues.description: Plan assumes the SELL->BUY-NO hotfix is already merged, but spot-check shows app/strategies/sentiment.py still returns SignalType.SELL for bearish aligned signals at app/strategies/sentiment.py:143. Because the hotfix is out of scope, PR-F could either ship the known bug or drift into unauthorized hotfix work.
issues.fix_proposed: Add a blocking preflight before PR-A: verify sentiment, event_driven, and knowledge_driven no longer emit strategy SELL for entry signals. If not true, stop and merge the separate hotfix first.

issues.description: Event exposure tracking is incomplete across close and restart. Current record_fill only knows token_id and size at app/risk/manager.py:215-233, record_close only knows token_id at app/risk/manager.py:234-243, and restore_from_store replays fills without event_id at app/execution/engine.py:742-759.
issues.fix_proposed: If PR-J is split into an authorized plan, track token_id -> event_id and token_id -> exposure, persist event_id with positions/trades, and restore event exposure on startup.

issues.description: check_order and record_fill remain separate operations, so concurrent tick invocations or parallel order paths can both pass an event cap before either records exposure. Current check is app/risk/manager.py:102-190 and fill is app/risk/manager.py:215-233.
issues.fix_proposed: Use an atomic reserve-and-record path guarded by an asyncio.Lock, or add an ExecutionEngine tick lock preventing concurrent ticks. Include a concurrency test that two same-event orders cannot both reserve over the cap.

issues.description: Mixed-category events are left unresolved. Splitting one event by category can produce multiple winners for the same event_id, directly conflicting with the fixed one-signal-per-group constraint. Registry domain filtering currently happens per domain at app/strategies/registry.py:30-42.
issues.fix_proposed: Freeze a deterministic policy: either fail closed for mixed-category events or perform category-specific strategy evaluation followed by one event-level final winner across all subgroups.

issues.location: PR-D, PR-E, PR-F, PR-G, PR-H, PR-I test steps
issues.description: Several migrated strategy test steps say 'analogo' or just 'Verifica' without concrete singleton regression cases. The prompt explicitly calls out migrated strategies without singleton-group regression as a risk. Existing strategy tests still call evaluate(market, valuation), e.g. tests/test_strategies/test_value_edge.py:60 and tests/test_strategies/test_rule_edge.py:95.
issues.fix_proposed: For every migrated strategy, list exact tests: singleton unchanged behavior, N>1 winner, all filtered -> None, tie by input order/id, missing valuation skipped, and legacy signature removed.

issues.location: PR-A step 3, PR-B step 5, Ambiguita A1
issues.description: Cache invalidation for event_id is unresolved. MarketService caches filtered Market objects at app/services/market_service.py:21-34 and app/services/market_service.py:47-72. If event membership is stale or an earlier cache entry has event_id == "", the plan does not define when to refresh events or invalidate market cache.
issues.fix_proposed: Use separate event cache TTL plus a grouping_version timestamp. When event cache refreshes, rebuild grouped market objects; never reuse cached event_id == "" after a successful /events refresh. Test stale empty event_id cache recovery.

issues.description: Engine result handling trusts returned signal.market_id. With the proposed group-level call, a buggy or legacy strategy can return a signal for a market outside the current group; the plan says to lookup mkt by signal.market_id but does not validate membership. Current code appends the loop market regardless of signal.market_id at app/execution/engine.py:245.
issues.fix_proposed: Before appending a signal, require sig.market_id in the current group and in valuations_subset. Drop and log invalid_strategy_signal otherwise. Add a test with a mock strategy returning an out-of-group market_id.

issues.description: Done conditions require Lessons.md to be updated, but .claude/tasks/lessons.md is outside the allowed implementation scope and unrelated to the group refactor.
issues.fix_proposed: Remove Lessons.md from done conditions or move it to a separate documentation task with explicit approval.
